_README: GPU VRAM tier -> vLLM model. Bootstrap reads this on each instance,
converts to GB, picks the highest tier whose `min_gb` <= reported VRAM.
AWQ quantized models picked by default for VRAM efficiency — vLLM supports
natively, ~4-bit quantization preserves 90-95% of full-precision quality.
Override with HOLOSCRIPT_AGENT_LOCAL_LLM_MODEL_OVERRIDE in agent.env if needed.
Inventory snapshot 2026-04-25 (29 running instances on Vast.ai):
140 GB H200          x1  -> 70b-tier (local-llm, Qwen 72B AWQ — switched from anthropic 2026-04-26 per cost discipline)

tiers.notes: AWQ q4 ~36GB. Production-grade reasoning + coding. Single instance class on current fleet (H100 NVL).
AWQ q4 ~9GB + KV cache. Fits 16GB cards. Strong on coding + structured output.
AWQ q4 ~5GB + KV cache. Fits 10-12GB cards. Decent for audits + tool-use.
fp16 ~6GB. Fits 7-8GB cards. Cheap classification + grep-summary tasks only.
Fallback for any GPU. Capacity-only — output quality too low for paper deliverables.
